domain: Anangu Pitjantjatjara tjukurpa Dreaming songline (central Australian desert; creator-ancestor paths across land+sky; waypoints + landmarks transmitted via ordered ceremonial traversal; hierarchical-age curriculum)
specific_mechanism: Continual-learning training schedule where training data is organized as an explicit graph G = (waypoints, edges) with each waypoint annotated by (a) topic/concept embedding, (b) hierarchical difficulty level d∈{novice, intermediate, mature}, (c) prerequisite-edges. Replay schedule walks the graph from low-d waypoints outward by BFS-with-difficulty-budget; revisits cycle on a temporal schedule.
llm_application: SONGLINE-CURRICULUM: a graph-traversal-ordered continual-learning training schedule. (1) Training corpus pre-clustered into a waypoint graph G = (V, E) with each waypoint v ∈ V annotated by topic-embedding, hierarchical difficulty d_v ∈ {1..D}, and prerequisite-edges to lower-d nodes. (2) Training proceeds via BFS-from-novice-frontier — start at d=1 waypoints, advance to d=2 only after BFS-frontier coverage of d=1 exceeds threshold τ. (3) Periodic 'inma replay' revisits a sampled subset of completed waypoints. (4) The graph itself is a transferable curriculum artifact across model checkpoints. Differs from Scalable CL with Replay 2505.12512 (replay-ratio consolidation without explicit waypoint-graph), CER ICLR 2025 (context-window experience replay without difficulty-graph), Waypoint-Trajectory Planning (waypoints as runtime plan, not training-data ordering), CL Survey 2603.12658 (CPT/DAP/CFT axes without graph traversal). Differs from R407 WANDJINA-SUPPRESSION (surgical output FT — different layer).
novelty_claim: Curriculum learning + replay-buffer continual learning + waypoint-graph-based training are all well-covered. SONGLINE-CURRICULUM adds (a) explicit difficulty-annotated waypoint graph + (b) BFS-from-novice replay schedule + (c) graph-as-transferable-curriculum-artifact. Recombination.
